# A Lyme Life

> Patient advocacy and treatment guidance for chronic Lyme disease by Christina Carter — a former chronic Lyme patient (misdiagnosed for 10 years) who guides patients and families to advanced treatment at trusted hospitals worldwide. Guidance is free to patients. All content is educational and experience-based, not medical advice. Christina is a patient advocate and educator, not a licensed medical provider.

Christina Carter was misdiagnosed for 10 years before her family received whole-body hyperthermia in Germany in 2017. She is a team member at The Lyme Specialist (since 2018) and serves on the Clinical Advisory Board of Lyme Re-code (Immunotherapy Institute). The site helps people who are still sick with chronic Lyme understand their options and find effective treatment.

## Start here — keystone guide
- [The Chronic Lyme Recovery Roadmap: From Diagnosis to Remission](https://alymelife.com/chronic-lyme-recovery-roadmap.html): The master hub for chronic Lyme recovery — a 5-stage roadmap (1: get clarity/diagnosis & team; 2: clear the infection; 3: reset the immune system incl. Treg therapy & MCAS; 4: support the whole you — detox, nutrition, nervous system, mood, mold; 5: build your new normal — recovery timeline, cost, disability). Links out to every in-depth guide on the site.

## Free tools & self-assessment
- [Is Whole-Body Hyperthermia Right for You? — Free Interactive Self-Assessment](https://alymelife.com/hyperthermia-self-assessment.html): A free, interactive 5-minute self-assessment. Answer 13 questions across 5 areas (treatment history, symptom burden, deep-seated/relapsing infection, immune reset, practical readiness) plus a cardiovascular safety screen; get an instant candidacy range (0–13 Foundation first, 14–26 Worth a conversation, 27–39 Strong candidate profile) and a printable result to bring to a clinician. Not a diagnosis.

## About & contact
- [About Christina Carter](https://alymelife.com/about.html): Bio, credentials, affiliations, and how her free patient-guidance works.
- [Home & free consultation](https://alymelife.com/): Christina's story, the treatments she guides patients to, and a free, no-pressure consultation request.

- [How to Find a Lyme-Literate Doctor or Treatment Center](https://alymelife.com/find-a-lyme-doctor.html): What LLMD means, where to look, questions to ask, and red flags to avoid.


## The Chronic Illness Chronicles (broader than Lyme)
An honest essay series on what chronic illness does to a whole family — relevant to Lyme, Long COVID, ME/CFS, MS, cancer, and autoimmune disease.
- [The Chronic Illness Chronicles (series hub)](https://alymelife.com/chronicles.html): What happens to families when health is stolen.
- [Part 0: When You're Ground Zero](https://alymelife.com/chronicles-ground-zero.html): For the patient — guilt, lost identity, and still being enough.
- [Part 1: When One Person Is Sick, the Whole House Feels It](https://alymelife.com/chronicles-whole-house.html): How chronic illness reshapes an entire household.
- [Part 2: What Chronic Illness Steals From Kids](https://alymelife.com/chronicles-the-kids.html): Children as the quiet caregivers.
- [Part 3: What Happens to the Ones Who Stay](https://alymelife.com/chronicles-the-spouse.html): Marriage and caregiving through chronic illness.
- [Part 4: What Now? How to Actually Live With It](https://alymelife.com/chronicles-what-now.html): Practical coping, the Spoon Theory, and finding joy.

## Comparisons
- [Extreme vs. Moderate Hyperthermia for Lyme](https://alymelife.com/blog-extreme-vs-moderate-hyperthermia.html): Side-by-side of the two forms of whole-body hyperthermia — extreme (~41.6–42°C, kill the infection under anesthesia) vs. moderate (~38.5–40°C, paired with Treg/immune therapy to retrain the immune system); temperatures, risks, cost, candidacy.
- [ArminLabs vs. IGeneX](https://alymelife.com/blog-arminlabs-vs-igenex.html): Side-by-side of the two specialty Lyme labs — IGeneX (US; antibody/ImmunoBlot, PCR) vs. ArminLabs (Germany; T-cellular EliSpot); antibody vs. T-cell testing, co-infection panels, ordering, cost.
- [LDN vs. Antibiotics for Lyme](https://alymelife.com/blog-ldn-vs-antibiotics.html): Why they do different jobs — antibiotics kill the infection (standard for early Lyme); low-dose naltrexone modulates the immune system and calms inflammation; often used together, not either/or.

## Understanding Lyme
- [Can Lyme Disease Kill You? An Honest Look at the Real Risks](https://alymelife.com/blog-can-lyme-disease-kill-you.html): Honest prognosis — death from Lyme is rare; covers Lyme carditis (heart block, the main life-threatening complication), co-infections, chronic-Lyme quality-of-life toll, life expectancy, "20 years later," and emergency red flags.
- [How to Detox from Mold: What Actually Worked for My Family](https://alymelife.com/blog-mold-detox.html): First-hand, practical mold-detox guide — remove exposure (remediate/leave), the belongings purge (keep metal/glass), open drainage first, binders (charcoal, bentonite, chlorella, cholestyramine) away from food/meds, sweating/heat, urine mycotoxin + ERMI testing, timeline. Based on the author's family's severe test-confirmed mold toxicity and six-month detox in Ecuador.
- [MCAS & Lyme: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome, Explained](https://alymelife.com/blog-mcas-lyme.html): What MCAS is (overreactive mast cells releasing histamine and other mediators), its multi-system symptoms (flushing/"MCAS face", skin, eyes, gut, brain fog, palpitations, flares), causes, whether it's autoimmune (no — immune dysregulation), the Lyme–mold–MCAS triad, diagnosis (tryptase, urinary markers, clinical pattern), and management (low-histamine diet, antihistamines/stabilizers, treating root drivers).

## Treatment guides
- [Buhner vs. Cowden Protocol for Lyme: A Practical Herbal Guide](https://alymelife.com/blog-herbal-protocols-lyme.html): The two best-known herbal protocols compared — Buhner (flexible, herb-by-herb: Japanese knotweed, cat's claw, andrographis, Chinese skullcap) vs. Cowden (standardized NutraMedix program: Samento, Banderol); plus a herb-by-herb primer (incl. cryptolepis) and honest caveats.
- [Lyme Recovery Timeline: 2 Years After Hyperthermia](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-recovery-timeline.html): An honest, phase-by-phase account of chronic Lyme recovery after whole-body hyperthermia — why healing isn't linear, the 9–14 month post-hyperthermia dip, reinfection, and what makes recovery hold (microbiome, immune rebalancing, pacing).
- [Whole-Body Hyperthermia for Lyme: A Patient's Complete Guide](https://alymelife.com/article.html): What hyperthermia is; extreme vs. moderate WBHT; how it works; who it helps; costs; risks; a treatment week abroad; reinfection.
- [Late-Stage & Chronic Lyme: Treatment Options When You're Still Sick](https://alymelife.com/chronic-lyme-treatment.html): The hub page — why Lyme becomes chronic, PTLDS, and every treatment option, linking to all guides below.
- [What Is Chronic Lyme? A Simple 3-Layer Framework](https://alymelife.com/blog-chronic-lyme-framework.html): Understanding chronic Lyme as infection + immune dysfunction + whole-body system breakdown, PTLDS, and why healing must be layered.
- [Autologous Treg Therapy for Lyme: When the Infection Is Gone but You're Still Sick](https://alymelife.com/treg-therapy.html): Autologous regulatory T-cell therapy for immune dysregulation after clearing Lyme; relevant after hyperthermia and for autoimmune symptoms, allergies, and new sensitivities.
- [The Wiegman Protocol for Lyme: Our Family's Journey](https://alymelife.com/blog-wiegman-protocol.html): The documented Wiegman Protocol experience with full video series.
- [Disulfiram & Azlocillin for Lyme: An Honest Patient's Guide](https://alymelife.com/blog-disulfiram-azlocillin.html): What these treatments are, what's proven vs. investigational, and the risks.
- [Stem Cell, SOT & Ozone: An Honest Guide to Alternative Lyme Therapies](https://alymelife.com/blog-alternative-therapies.html): SOT, stem cell, ozone, IV therapies and apheresis — what to expect and how to vet a clinic.
- [Natural & Holistic Treatments for Lyme: What Helps (and What Doesn't)](https://alymelife.com/blog-natural-remedies.html): Herbs, homeopathy, cryptolepis, supplements, diet, detox, ozone — honest verdicts on each.
- [When Antibiotics Stop Working for Chronic Lyme](https://alymelife.com/blog-antibiotics.html): Why standard antibiotic courses fall short for persistent Lyme.
- [How to Treat Lyme Without Endless Antibiotics](https://alymelife.com/blog-treat-lyme-without-antibiotics.html): Why antibiotics fail for chronic Lyme and the layered alternative approach \u2014 hyperthermia, immune reset, detox, mitochondrial repair.
- [TUDCA for Lyme Recovery](https://alymelife.com/blog-tudca.html): How the bile acid TUDCA may support the brain, liver, gut, and mitochondria in Lyme recovery (it does not kill Borrelia).
- [LDN (Low-Dose Naltrexone) for Lyme](https://alymelife.com/blog-ldn-lyme.html): How low-dose naltrexone is used off-label for chronic-Lyme pain, fatigue, brain fog, and immune dysregulation — dosing, the opioid safety rule, and honest evidence (it does not kill Borrelia).
- [Lyme Detox Enemas: Coffee, Herbal & Water](https://alymelife.com/blog-detox-enemas.html): How coffee, herbal, and water enemas are used as detox support during Lyme die-off, plus cautions.

## Symptoms & co-infections
- [Lyme Disease Symptoms: Early Signs, the Rash & Unusual Symptoms](https://alymelife.com/lyme-disease-symptoms.html): Early, late, and unusual symptoms; the bullseye-rash myth; symptoms by stage.
- [I Just Got Bitten by a Tick — What Do I Do Right Now?](https://alymelife.com/blog-tick-bite-what-to-do.html): Step-by-step after a tick bite — removal, saving the tick, symptoms to watch, and why not to just wait for a test.
- [Lyme Rage: When the Illness Changes Who You Are](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-rage.html): Why sudden anger and irritability (often Bartonella-driven) are real physical symptoms, and how to cope.
- [Is It in My Head? Lyme, Anxiety & Depression](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-anxiety-depression.html): How Lyme and co-infections cause psychiatric symptoms via neuroinflammation, and why to treat both.
- [Lyme Brain: What Brain Fog Actually Feels Like](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-brain-fog.html): What Lyme brain fog feels like, why it happens, how to cope, and whether it improves.
- [Mold + Lyme: The Connection No One Warned You About](https://alymelife.com/blog-mold-and-lyme.html): Why mold illness (CIRS) and Lyme overlap and feed each other, and why you may need to address both.
- [Neurological Lyme Disease: Symptoms, Brain Fog & the Nervous System](https://alymelife.com/blog-neurological-lyme.html): Brain fog, facial palsy, nerve pain, and the psychiatric side of Lyme.
- [Unusual Symptoms of Lyme Disease: The Weird Signs Doctors Miss](https://alymelife.com/blog-unusual-lyme-symptoms.html): The strange, easily-dismissed symptoms of Lyme and why the pattern matters more than any list.
- [Lyme Disease Symptoms in Women: Why It Looks Different & Gets Missed](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-symptoms-in-women.html): How Lyme symptoms differ in women, the hormonal connection, and why women are so often misdiagnosed.
- [The Stages of Lyme Disease: Early, Disseminated & Late Symptoms](https://alymelife.com/blog-stages-of-lyme.html): The three stages of Lyme and the symptoms of each, including stage 3 neurological Lyme.
- [Bell's Palsy from Lyme Disease: Symptoms & Living With It](https://alymelife.com/blog-bells-palsy-lyme.html): Why Lyme causes facial palsy, the symptoms, whether it resolves, and living with lasting facial droop.
- [Bartonella: Symptoms, the Telltale Rash & Treatment](https://alymelife.com/blog-bartonella.html): Bartonella symptoms, the stretch-mark-like rash, the neuropsychiatric link, and treatment.
- [Babesia: Symptoms, Treatment & the Lyme Connection](https://alymelife.com/blog-babesia.html): Air hunger, night sweats, and the malaria-like parasite that Lyme antibiotics won't touch.
- [Co-infections 101: Bartonella, Babesia & Why They Matter](https://alymelife.com/blog-coinfections.html): Overview of the co-infections that keep people sick after treating Lyme.
- [The Herxheimer Reaction ("Herx"): What It Is & How to Cope](https://alymelife.com/blog-herxheimer-reaction.html): Why treatment can make you feel worse before better, and how to ease it.
- [Lyme, POTS & Dysautonomia](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-pots-dysautonomia.html): Why chronic Lyme and Bartonella can cause POTS and dysautonomia — racing heart and dizziness on standing, how POTS is diagnosed (tilt-table/NASA lean), and what helps (fluids, salt, compression, recumbent exercise, medications).
- [PANS, PANDAS & Lyme in Children](https://alymelife.com/blog-pans-pandas-lyme.html): When a child changes overnight (sudden OCD, tics, rage, food refusal), tick-borne infections like Lyme and Bartonella can be the trigger — hallmark signs, why it's misdiagnosed, and the three-pronged treatment approach.

## Testing, diagnosis & experience
- [The Best Lyme Disease Tests: Why Standard Testing Misses It (ArminLabs & More)](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-testing.html): Why standard tests miss Lyme, the test types, and specialty labs (ArminLabs, IGeneX, Galaxy/Nanotrap).
- [Misdiagnosed with Lyme for Years: My Story](https://alymelife.com/blog-misdiagnosed.html): A decade of misdiagnosis and what finally helped.
- [The Great Imitator: 5 Diseases Often Mistaken for Lyme](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-great-imitator.html): Why Lyme is misdiagnosed as fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue syndrome, MS, rheumatoid arthritis, and lupus \u2014 with prevalence stats and why standard testing misses it.

## Practical: cost, funding, travel, diet
- [What Lyme Treatment Abroad Really Costs](https://alymelife.com/blog-cost.html): Honest budgeting for treatment abroad, plus insurance-recovery help.
- [How to Pay for Lyme Treatment: Funding, Grants & Financial Help](https://alymelife.com/blog-treatment-funding.html): Grants, crowdfunding, insurance recovery, and financing.
- [Getting Disability for Chronic Lyme (SSDI/SSI)](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-disability.html): A realistic guide to applying for U.S. Social Security disability with chronic Lyme — why claims are denied, what evidence wins one (RFC forms, neuropsych testing, function logs), SSDI vs. SSI, and the appeal ladder.
- [Traveling to Germany for Hyperthermia: What to Expect](https://alymelife.com/blog-germany.html): Practical guidance for treatment travel to Germany.
- [Questions to Ask Before Choosing a Lyme Treatment Center](https://alymelife.com/blog-choosing-center.html): How to vet a clinic and choose the right center.
- [The Lyme Disease Diet: What to Eat (and Avoid)](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-diet.html): An anti-inflammatory diet for Lyme, plus eating well with fatigue.

## More guides on the site
- [Lyme Disease Treatment Centers Compared: How to Choose](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-treatment-centers.html): A side-by-side comparison pillar of the leading Lyme treatment centers and clinics — what each is known for, treatment models, and how to choose the right fit.
- [Sanoviv Medical Institute Review: A Lyme Patient's Honest Look](https://alymelife.com/blog-sanoviv-review.html): An honest review of Sanoviv Medical Institute for chronic Lyme — approach, what to expect, cost, and who it suits.
- [The Lyme Disease Rash: What a Bull's-Eye Really Looks Like](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-disease-rash.html): What the erythema migrans / bull's-eye rash actually looks like, how often it appears, and the many Lyme rashes that aren't bull's-eyes.
- [Bull's-Eye Rash but Negative Lyme Test? What It Means](https://alymelife.com/blog-bullseye-rash-negative-test.html): Why you can have a rash and still test negative early, why the rash alone can justify treatment, and what to do next.
- [Can Lyme Disease Come Back? Relapse & Reinfection Explained](https://alymelife.com/blog-can-lyme-come-back.html): Why Lyme can relapse or be caught again, the difference between relapse and reinfection, and how to protect a hard-won recovery.
- [Alpha-Gal Syndrome: The Tick-Borne Red-Meat Allergy](https://alymelife.com/blog-alpha-gal-syndrome.html): What alpha-gal syndrome is, how a tick bite triggers a delayed red-meat allergy, symptoms, testing, and living with it.
- [Lyme Disease Symptoms in Children: What Parents Miss](https://alymelife.com/blog-lyme-symptoms-in-children.html): How Lyme looks different in kids, why it's so easily missed, behavioral and physical signs, and the PANS/PANDAS overlap.
- [Is Lyme Disease Contagious? What You Need to Know](https://alymelife.com/blog-is-lyme-contagious.html): Whether Lyme spreads person-to-person, sexually, congenitally, or through blood, and how transmission actually works.
- [How to Get Your Doctor to Take Lyme Seriously](https://alymelife.com/blog-doctor-take-lyme-seriously.html): A practical script for being believed — how to prepare, what to bring, and what to do when a doctor dismisses chronic Lyme.

## Video library
- [Watch & Learn video library](https://alymelife.com/videos.html): Christina's videos and playlists — hyperthermia, treatment in Germany, post-hyperthermia recovery updates, Wiegman Protocol, stem cell, FAQs, recipes, and motivation.

## Topics covered
chronic Lyme disease; late-stage Lyme; PTLDS; whole-body hyperthermia (extreme and moderate); autologous Treg therapy; immune dysregulation; Lyme co-infections (Bartonella, Babesia); Lyme symptoms and neurological Lyme; brain fog; Herxheimer reaction; Lyme testing (ArminLabs, IGeneX, Galaxy/Nanotrap); disulfiram, azlocillin, and the Wiegman Protocol; alternative therapies (stem cell, SOT, ozone); natural and holistic treatment; treatment abroad (Germany, Mexico, Switzerland); cost, funding, and insurance; the Lyme diet; misdiagnosis; reinfection.

## Important note
Christina Carter is a patient advocate and educator, not a licensed medical provider. Everything on the site is for educational purposes only and is not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Individual results vary. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional.

## Contact
Free, no-pressure consultation: https://alymelife.com/#contact
